zotero.abstractNote: Synaptic plasticity rules are often inferred from experimental data by assuming a specific functional form. However, it is unclear whether different plasticity rules can lead to the same network-level dynamics. Here, we show that indistinguishable network dynamics can emerge from unalike plasticity rules. We use a simulation-based inference framework to identify the space of plasticity rules that give rise to the same network-level dynamics. We find that a wide range of plasticity rules can produce the same network-level dynamics, suggesting that inferring plasticity rules from network-level observations is an ill-posed problem. Our results highlight the importance of considering the non-uniqueness of plasticity rules when interpreting experimental data and designing artificial neural networks.
